summary: Problem 9 asks for an explicit, degree-bounded, A-independent polynomial map F characterizing algebraic relations on scaled determinantal tensors Q^(αβγδ) built from generic 3×4 matrices, with an if-and-only-if proof. The current state has no evaluable proof attempt — the sole issue is a closed seed/placeholder tracker — and a low 16% solvability score reflecting the genuine research-level difficulty.
problems: No actual proof exists: the only issue is a placeholder/seed tracker that was closed with 'no proof to evaluate', so there is nothing substantive to assess.
Core mathematical blocker: constructing an explicit polynomial map F on the scaled determinantal tensors that is provably independent of the matrices A^(i) and has bounded degree.
Missing the hard direction — a full if-and-only-if characterization linking vanishing of F to the existence of valid A^(1),...,A^(n) realizing the tensors.
Discrepancy in tracking: 'Best Proof' reports 100 open issues while the issue list shows only 1 (resolved), indicating unreliable status metadata.
Genericity (Zariski-generic A) and the n≥5 dimensional regime need a rigorous handling that current materials do not address.
highlights: The problem statement and setup are clearly specified, including dimensions (3×4 inputs, 3×3×3×3 tensors), the genericity assumption, and the n≥5 regime.
Provenance is well-documented (Joe Kileel, UT Austin; First_Proof.tex source repository), aiding reproducibility.
The solvability assessment correctly identifies the precise deliverable: an explicit, degree-bounded, A-independent map F plus a biconditional proof.

suggested_todos.title: Draft an initial construction of the polynomial map F on scaled determinantal tensors and verify A-independence on small cases (n=5)
Establish the 'easy' direction: prove that valid realizations imply F vanishes, with explicit degree bounds
Open a substantive proof-tracking issue to replace the closed seed/placeholder so progress can be evaluated
Reconcile conflicting issue counts (100 open vs 1 resolved) in the status metadata
Survey Kileel's related work on determinantal tensor relations to source candidate techniques for the converse direction
